title: RAG Architecture Foundations Capstone
concept: Assembling indexing, retrieval, and grounded generation into one complete RAG system diagram; 'Next up: Advanced RAG Patterns.'.

exercises.prompt: In our last lesson, we looked at the trade-off between faithfulness and fluency. How does a highly faithful system behave?

options.text: It strictly sticks to the facts in the provided text, even if it sounds terse.
It prioritizes poetic flow over factual correctness.

exercises.explain: A highly faithful model prioritizes factual accuracy based on the provided documents, even if it makes the output sound a bit plain or repetitive.

cards.title: The Grand Blueprint
cards.body: We have explored how LLMs find facts and how they generate text. Now, we are going to glue all these pieces together into one grand blueprint that powers modern AI search engines!

cards.title: RAG Architecture
cards.body: A RAG architecture connects external document databases to an LLM. It operates in two main phases: an offline phase that slices and stores documents, and an online phase that fetches matching documents to guide the LLM's final response.

cards.title: The Smart Library System
cards.body: Think of a RAG system like a high-tech library assistant. Indexing is stocking and organizing the bookshelves, retrieval is the assistant running to grab the three best books, and generation is the assistant writing a neat summary of those pages for you.

cards.title: RAG in Action
cards.body: When a user asks 'How do I return a device?', the RAG system finds the 'Return Policy' PDF in its database, pastes that policy directly into a prompt template, and asks the LLM to write a polite email reply using only that policy.

cards.title: No Training Required!
cards.body: Many think RAG requires retraining or fine-tuning the LLM itself. In reality, the LLM remains completely untouched; we simply feed it the right documents at the exact moment it needs them.

cards.title: Two Sides of RAG
cards.body: Let's compare the two active phases of a RAG system: the offline setup and the live user query phase.

left.text: Splitting documents into chunks and saving them in a vector database for quick search later.

right.text: Taking a user query, retrieving matching chunks, and feeding them to the LLM to get an answer.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following actions represents the retrieval stage in a RAG system?

options.text: Chunking and storing documents in a database
Searching the database to find matching text snippets for a query
Writing the final conversational response to the user
Translating the user query into multiple languages

exercises.explain: Retrieval is the active, online step where the system queries the database to find the most relevant document chunks for the user's question.

exercises.prompt: True or False: If the retrieval step fails to find the right documents, the generation step cannot produce a grounded answer.
exercises.explain: If the retrieval system fails or finds irrelevant documents, the generator will not have the correct facts to work with, leading to an incorrect or ungrounded answer.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing step of the pipeline.
exercises.sentence: Before we can search our documents, we must prepare them using indexing.
exercises.explain: The indexing step processes and stores documents beforehand so they can be quickly searched later.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario shows a system using a complete RAG pipeline?

options.text: An engineer manually copies text from a website and pastes it into ChatGPT.
A chatbot answers a medical question using only the pre-trained knowledge inside its weights.
A customer support bot automatically searches a wiki database for the user's query and inserts the top article into the LLM's prompt.

exercises.explain: Option C describes an automated pipeline combining a retrieval search step with an LLM prompt context, which is the definition of a RAG pipeline. Options A and B are manual or non-grounded.

exercises.prompt: You are building a complete, end-to-end RAG system from scratch. What is the correct logical sequence of stages that data and queries flow through to produce a grounded response?

options.text: Indexing -> Retrieval -> Prompt Augmentation -> LLM Generation
LLM Generation -> Indexing -> Retrieval -> Prompt Augmentation
Retrieval -> LLM Generation -> Indexing -> User Feedback
Prompt Augmentation -> LLM Generation -> Evaluation -> Indexing

exercises.explain: A complete RAG system is a pipeline where documents are first indexed (pre-processed and stored), then relevant chunks are retrieved based on a query, and finally, those chunks are sent with the query to the LLM to generate a grounded answer. Next up: Advanced RAG Patterns.
